patterns.name: SSL/TLS Bypass (Node.js)
patterns.remediation: Remove SSL bypass. Fix the underlying certificate issue instead. If needed for corporate proxy, document in allowlist with justification.

patterns.name: SSL/TLS Bypass (Python)
patterns.remediation: Enable SSL verification. Install proper CA certificates.

patterns.name: SSL/TLS Bypass (curl)
patterns.description: curl command with SSL verification bypassed
patterns.remediation: Remove -k/--insecure flag. Fix certificate trust chain.

patterns.name: Debug Mode Enabled
patterns.description: Debug mode enabled — may expose internal information
patterns.remediation: Disable debug mode for production. Use environment-based config.

patterns.name: Default Credentials
patterns.description: Default or well-known credential pair detected
patterns.remediation: Remove default credentials. Use strong, unique passwords via secret manager.

patterns.name: Permissive CORS
patterns.description: CORS allows all origins — potential cross-origin attack vector
patterns.remediation: Restrict CORS to specific allowed origins.

patterns.name: Disabled Authentication
patterns.remediation: Enable authentication. Use proper auth mechanisms.

patterns.name: World-Readable Permissions
patterns.remediation: Use restrictive permissions (chmod 700 or 600). Apply principle of least privilege.

patterns.name: HTTP in Production Config
patterns.description: Non-HTTPS URL in configuration — data transmitted in plaintext
patterns.remediation: Use HTTPS for all external connections.
